description:
<title>Generate a consolidated vulnerability report from provided Nmap XML data</title>

    <story>
        You are working as a penetration tester.  A colleague already performed an Nmap version scan and saved the raw XML output in:

        • /home/user/scan_data/initial_scan.xml

        They also produced a simple mapping of service-versions to a single representative CVE, stored in:

        • /home/user/scan_data/cve_mapping.csv

        Management now wants a concise, machine-readable report that lists every open port discovered, the exact software running on it, and the corresponding CVE.  Your job is to parse the XML, join it with the mapping, and save the final report in your own working directory.
    </story>

    <requirements>
        1. Make a new directory called “/home/user/vuln_scan”.  
           • If the directory already exists, reuse it (do not delete anything outside it).  

        2. Read /home/user/scan_data/initial_scan.xml and extract, for every <port> element that is marked as “open”, the following fields in exactly this order:  
           a. host_ip  – the IPv4 address found in the <address addr="…"/> element of that host  
           b. port      – the value of portid (as an integer, no quotes)  
           c. protocol  – the protocol attribute (e.g. “tcp”)  
           d. service   – the service “product” attribute text exactly as it appears in the file (do NOT fall back to the “name” attribute; use “product”)  
           e. version   – the service “version” attribute text exactly as it appears in the file  
        
        3. Using the mapping file /home/user/scan_data/cve_mapping.csv, add a sixth column “cve_reference” that corresponds to the row where both the service (column “service”) AND version (column “version”) match exactly (case-sensitive) the values you just extracted.  
           • If no match exists, use the literal text “N/A”.  
           • There are *no* unmatched rows in the provided data set, but still implement the fallback.

        4. Write the final, consolidated list to a new file called /home/user/vuln_scan/vulnerability_report.csv with **precisely** this specification:  
           • Comma-separated values (CSV)  
           • NO header row  
           • One line per open port, in the natural order they appear in the XML file (top→bottom).  
           • Exactly six fields per line, in this fixed order:  
             host_ip,port,protocol,service,version,cve_reference  
           • Each line must end with a single Unix newline (LF).
        
        5. Create a small process log at /home/user/vuln_scan/scan_steps.log.  
           • The log must contain exactly three lines, each documenting one major phase of your script:  
             PARSE_XML, MERGE_CVE, WRITE_REPORT  
           • Each line should start with a timestamp in the format: [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] followed by a single space and then the literal text  
             STEP:<step_name>  (example:  [2024-05-15 14:03:22] STEP:PARSE_XML)  
           • No extra lines before or after those three.

        6. When everything is complete, you should be able to run the following commands and see the exact output (note: the testing harness will run something similar):  

           cat /home/user/vuln_scan/vulnerability_report.csv  
           cat /home/user/vuln_scan/scan_steps.log  

           The tester will compare those contents byte-for-byte with the expected data, so be meticulous about ordering, commas, and newlines.
    </requirements>

    <hints>
        • You can rely solely on common POSIX utilities (grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, etc.) or any installed Python 3 interpreter—whichever is easier.  
        • Remember that the “product” attribute in the XML contains spaces (e.g. “Apache httpd”), so mind your field extraction.  
        • The mapping file is an ordinary CSV with a header row.  
        • Every open port in the XML has an exact match in the mapping file—no need for fuzzy matching.  
        • Make sure the output directory exists before writing files.  
        • Be consistent with line endings: the grader runs in a Unix container and expects LF only.  
        • Double-check the final report manually with `cat` before finishing.
    </hints>
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files provided for the agent -->
    <file path="/home/user/scan_data/initial_scan.xml"><![CDATA[
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<nmaprun>
 <host>
  <address addr="192.168.56.10" addrtype="ipv4"/>
  <ports>
   <port protocol="tcp" portid="22">
    <state state="open"/>
    <service name="ssh" product="OpenSSH" version="7.2p2"/>
   </port>
   <port protocol="tcp" portid="80">
    <state state="open"/>
    <service name="http" product="Apache httpd" version="2.4.18"/>
   </port>
  </ports>
 </host>
 <host>
  <address addr="192.168.56.12" addrtype="ipv4"/>
  <ports>
   <port protocol="tcp" portid="21">
    <state state="open"/>
    <service name="ftp" product="vsftpd" version="3.0.3"/>
   </port>
   <port protocol="tcp" portid="445">
    <state state="open"/>
    <service name="microsoft-ds" product="Samba smbd" version="3.6.25"/>
   </port>
  </ports>
 </host>
 <host>
  <address addr="192.168.56.15" addrtype="ipv4"/>
  <ports>
   <port protocol="tcp" portid="3306">
    <state state="open"/>
    <service name="mysql" product="MySQL" version="5.5.49"/>
   </port>
  </ports>
 </host>
</nmaprun>
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/scan_data/cve_mapping.csv"><![CDATA[
service,version,cve_reference
OpenSSH,7.2p2,CVE-2016-6515
Apache httpd,2.4.18,CVE-2017-3167
vsftpd,3.0.3,CVE-2015-1419
Samba smbd,3.6.25,CVE-2017-7494
MySQL,5.5.49,CVE-2016-6662
]]></file>

    <!-- The directory that must eventually exist -->
    <directory path="/home/user/vuln_scan" mode="755"/>

    <!-- Expected artifacts after successful completion -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/vuln_scan/vulnerability_report.csv" mode="644"><![CDATA[
192.168.56.10,22,tcp,OpenSSH,7.2p2,CVE-2016-6515
192.168.56.10,80,tcp,Apache httpd,2.4.18,CVE-2017-3167
192.168.56.12,21,tcp,vsftpd,3.0.3,CVE-2015-1419
192.168.56.12,445,tcp,Samba smbd,3.6.25,CVE-2017-7494
192.168.56.15,3306,tcp,MySQL,5.5.49,CVE-2016-6662
]]></expected_file>

    <expected_file path="/home/user/vuln_scan/scan_steps.log" mode="644">
        <!-- The timestamp will vary, so the test harness will ignore the actual numbers and only check the three step tokens in order.  
             Regex: ^\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\] STEP:(PARSE_XML|MERGE_CVE|WRITE_REPORT)$ -->
    </expected_file>
name: task_000000_0bda426e
